channel.title: Contributing to Meshery Schemas on Documentation
channel.description: Recent content in Contributing to Meshery Schemas on Documentation

item.title: Schema Fundamentals
item.description:
Meshery uses Schema-Driven Development (SDD): every API resource is defined once as an OpenAPI schema in meshery/schemas. That single definition propagates automatically into Go structs, TypeScript types, RTK Query clients, and runtime validation. Changes to a schema file update all downstream consumers on the next make build.

Schema propagation 

schemas/constructs/v1beta1/<construct>/
├── <construct>.yaml ← entity definition
└── api.yml ← endpoints + Payload schemas
 │
 ▼ make build
 bundle-openapi
 │
 ├─▶ generate-golang → models/v1beta1/<construct>/<construct>.go
 ├─▶ generate-ts → typescript/generated/v1beta1/<Construct>/<Construct>.ts
 └─▶ generate-rtk → typescript/rtk/ (RTK Query hooks)

The pipeline runs in four stages:

item.title: Authoring a Schema
item.description:
This page walks through the full lifecycle of adding or modifying a schema construct: directory layout, entity definition, Payload schema, API endpoints, template files, Go helpers, TypeScript exports, and the pre-PR checklist.

Read schema fundamentals first

Before authoring, read Schema Fundamentals for the dual-schema pattern, casing rules, and HTTP design principles.

Before you start

Confirm the tools are available (see the Prerequisites section in the index for the authoritative versions):

item.title: Schema Rules and Extensions
item.description:
The meshery/schemas repository enforces a set of validation rules via the validation/ Go package. Rules in the 1–36 range are blocking and must pass before a PR can merge. Rules in the 37–42 range are advisory and reported separately.

Validator CLI targets 

make validate-schemas # blocking rules only (CI gate)
make validate-schemas-strict # all rules including advisory
make audit-schemas # advisory report — new issues only vs baseline
make audit-schemas-full # full advisory backlog
make consumer-audit # check downstream consumers (meshery/meshery, layer5io/meshery-cloud)

Run make validate-schemas && make consumer-audit before every PR.

item.title: Schema Consumers and Build
item.description:
This page covers the downstream artifacts produced by make build and how each consumer — Go, TypeScript, and the RTK Query layer — uses them.

Build pipeline 

schemas/constructs/**/*.yaml, api.yml ← you write these
 │
 ▼ make bundle-openapi
 _openapi_build/
 ├── merged_openapi.yml
 ├── cloud_openapi.yml ← x-internal: ["cloud"] ops only
 └── meshery_openapi.yml ← x-internal: ["meshery"] ops only
 │
 ├─▶ make generate-golang
 │ └── models/v1beta1/<construct>/<construct>.go
 │
 ├─▶ make generate-ts
 │ └── typescript/generated/v1beta1/<Construct>/
 │ ├── <Construct>.ts (TypeScript types)
 │ └── <Construct>Schema.ts (schema as const JS object)
 │
 └─▶ make generate-rtk
 └── typescript/rtk/ (RTK Query hooks)

Makefile targets

Target What it does
make setup go mod tidy + npm install
make build Full build: validate + bundle + Go + TypeScript + RTK + tests
make bundle-openapi Resolve $ref and merge specs into _openapi_build/
make generate-golang Generate Go structs (requires bundle-openapi first)
make generate-ts Generate TypeScript types and schema objects
make generate-rtk Generate RTK Query client hooks
make validate-schemas Run blocking validation rules (CI gate)
make validate-schemas-strict Run all 42 rules including advisory
make audit-schemas Advisory report — new issues only vs baseline
make audit-schemas-full Full advisory backlog
make consumer-audit Check downstream consumers for casing conformance
npm run build Build TypeScript distribution (dist/)
go test ./... Run all Go tests

What NOT to commit

Path Generated by Do not commit
models/<version>/<pkg>/<pkg>.go make generate-golang Yes
typescript/generated/ make generate-ts Yes
dist/ npm run build Yes
_openapi_build/ make bundle-openapi Yes
Path Manually maintained Commit
schemas/constructs/**/api.yml Author Yes
schemas/constructs/**/<construct>.yaml Author Yes
schemas/constructs/**/templates/ Author Yes
typescript/index.ts Author Yes
models/<version>/<pkg>/<pkg>_helper.go Author Yes
models/core/datatype_*.go Author Yes

Go consumers

Generated structs

make generate-golang runs oapi-codegen against the bundled spec and writes Go struct files to models/v1beta1/<construct>/<construct>.go. The generation pipeline has several post-processing steps:

item.title: Schema-Driven UI Development
item.description:
Meshery aims to decouple UI logic from hardcoded structures so the UI dynamically adapts when schemas change, without requiring constant manual updates. This page explains how to consume schema definitions from the meshery/schemas repository in the Meshery UI.

Overview

Meshery’s schemas define the structure of data (constructs) using JSON Schema and their API operations using OpenAPI specs. From these definitions, Meshery auto-generates:

  • Go structs for backend validation and API handling
  • TypeScript types and objects for frontend development
  • RTK Query hooks for type-safe data fetching
  • Templates in JSON and YAML formats

The schema is the single source of truth — the UI should read types, validation rules, form metadata, and component props from the generated TypeScript package, not from hardcoded constants.
